session.name: Local model download slowdown investigation during LTX-2.3 install

goal.original: Investigate model download UI speed flicker, then diagnose severe local download slowdown during LTX-2.3 model install.
goal.summary: Speed flicker was fixed and committed. Local model dependency downloads were capped to one active file and committed. A follow-up low-speed reconnect watchdog was added because a single long-lived Hugging Face/Xet stream still degraded from tens of MB/s to ~100-300 KB/s while a fresh ranged request to the same file reached ~10.3 MB/s.

completed: Diagnosed original speed label flicker as per-dependency instantaneous speed being shown on the model card during multi-dep downloads.
Committed d58eb4b: Stabilize model download speed display.
Confirmed local _startPendingDeps previously started every queued dep immediately.
Committed 47e924a: Limit local model dependency downloads.
After restart, confirmed local scheduler used 0/1 active with ltx23-gemma-clip downloading and ltx23-lora-talkvid queued.
Confirmed remaining slowdown was real byte movement, not UI noise, and not caused by parallel local deps.
Header probe showed Hugging Face resolver quota was not exhausted (RateLimit r=2998 of q=3000), and the file was served via Xet/CDN with byte ranges.
Fresh curl ranged probe against the same active file downloaded 32 MB at about 10.3 MB/s while the app's long-lived stream was around 0.1-0.3 MB/s.
Committed 02ef2c0: Recover degraded model download streams.
pending: User will pause, close the app, restart, and resume to test the low-speed reconnect watchdog in a fresh server process.
Watch logs for 'slow-stream reconnect for ltx23-gemma-clip' if speed collapses after initially healthy throughput.
Decide whether to keep local concurrency at 1, make it configurable, or specialize by host after real-world testing.
Update .claude/rules/downloads.md and docs/comfy.md only after user approval, because architectural rule files are already dirty from unrelated work.

task_card.title: LTX-2.3 app integration — stand up the LTX video model family (workflow-gen + deps + audio modes UI)

completed: Legacy docs handoff pointer written under docs/handoffs/.
pending: If the low-speed reconnect watchdog works, document local-download concurrency and stream recovery in .claude/rules/downloads.md and docs/comfy.md after explicit user approval.
Consider writing a memory entry about Hugging Face/Xet long-lived local streams degrading while fresh ranged connections remain fast.
Do not change RunPod remote wrapper download behavior unless testing shows a separate cloud regression; user reports cloud downloads are extremely fast.

next_action.description: After the user restarts the app and resumes the download, inspect logs/app.log and /comfy/downloads/status to verify whether the slow-stream watchdog fires and whether throughput recovers.

key_decisions: Local and RunPod download paths must be treated separately. The local path uses node-downloader-helper in routes/downloadManager.js; the RunPod path uses the remote wrapper event stream and has been performing well.
Do not assume Hugging Face formal rate limiting was the issue. Live headers showed resolver quota available, while a new ranged connection was fast.
The local scheduler cap was kept local-only by changing _startPendingDeps/ResumableDownloader behavior, not remoteModels wrapper behavior.
The speed-display smoothing remains useful even with sequential local downloads because progress ticks and degraded streams can still be jittery.
constraints: Project rules require reading CLAUDE.md, .claude/rules/dos_and_donts.md, .claude/rules/downloads.md, docs/PROJECT.md, and logs/app.log tail before further download code changes.
Do not update .claude/rules/ without explicit user approval.
Current app process must be restarted before commit 02ef2c0 can affect the active download.

Unrelated dirty files exist in the worktree, including .claude/rules/downloads.md, .claude/rules/comfy_injection.md, MPI-127 task files, model/prompt/command files, and prompt box files. Do not include or revert them accidentally.

resume_prompt: Read .agents/mpi-kanban/state/handoffs/7528c51c-cb88-448f-8f53-3a29940668f4.json and use mpi-continue to continue from where we left off. You are continuing MPI-127 context, but the immediate focus is the local model download slowdown follow-up: after the user restarts the app and resumes the LTX-2.3 download, inspect logs/app.log and /comfy/downloads/status to verify whether the low-speed reconnect watchdog fires and whether throughput recovers.
